exercises.title: Trace inherited method call
exercises.scenario: Understand that subclasses inherit parent methods.
hints: Car extends Vehicle → Car inherits describe()
Car constructor calls super('Car') → sets type = 'Car'
c.describe() uses Vehicle's describe() with type = 'Car'

exercises.title: Write a subclass with super()
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete a Dog that extends Animal.
hints: `extends Animal` establishes the inheritance
`super(name)` calls Animal's constructor
Must be first line of constructor

exercises.title: Shape hierarchy
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Shape` base class with private field color (String). Constructor + getter. Subclass `Circle` adds radius (double). Constructor calls super. Method `area()` returns πr².

In main, create and print a circle's color and area.

Expected output:
Color: red
Area: 78.53981633974483
hints: Circle constructor: super(color) first, then this.radius = radius
getColor() is inherited from Shape
area() is Circle-specific
Math.PI * radius * radius

exercises.title: Override a method
exercises.instructions:
Create an `Animal` class with makeSound() that prints 'Animal makes a sound'. Create `Dog` and `Cat` that each override makeSound() with their specific sound.

In main, create one of each and call makeSound().

Expected output:
Woof!
Meow!
exercises.starter_code: // Create Animal, Dog, Cat — override makeSound in each subclass
hints: @Override annotation before the overriding method
Same method signature: public void makeSound()
Each subclass provides its own body

exercises.title: Fix missing super() call
exercises.instructions:
This code doesn't compile. Fix it.

Expected output:
Buddy the Labrador

bugs.issue: Dog's constructor doesn't call super(). Animal has no no-arg constructor. Java can't initialize the Animal part.
bugs.fix: Add super(name) as the first line of Dog's constructor

hints: Animal only has Animal(String name) — no no-arg constructor
Dog must call super(name) explicitly
super() must be the first line of the constructor

exercises.title: Use super.method() in override
exercises.scenario: Extend parent's behavior without rewriting it.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Person` class with method intro() that prints 'I am [name]'. Create a `Student` that overrides intro() to ALSO print their major — use super.intro() to reuse the parent's message.

Expected output:
I am Aisha
I am Aisha, studying Computer Science
exercises.starter_code: // Create Person and Student — Student.intro() uses super.intro()

test_cases.expected_stdout:
I am Aisha
I am Aisha, studying Computer Science

hints: Student.intro() calls super.intro() first
Then adds its own line
super.intro() calls Person's version — no repetition

exercises.title: Three-level inheritance chain
exercises.scenario: Multiple levels of inheritance working together.
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `LivingThing`: breathe() prints 'Breathing'
- `Animal extends LivingThing`: eat() prints '<name> eats'
- `Dog extends Animal`: fetch() prints '<name> fetches'

In main, create a Dog that calls all three methods.

Expected output:
Breathing
Rex eats
Rex fetches
hints: Dog extends Animal, Animal extends LivingThing
Dog inherits from ALL levels: breathe, eat, AND fetch
Each super() call chains up
Method lookup: Dog → Animal → LivingThing

exercises.title: Array of parent type, mixed subclass objects
exercises.scenario: Polymorphism preview — treating different subclasses uniformly.
exercises.instructions:
Using Animal (with name + makeSound()), Dog (overrides: Woof!), Cat (overrides: Meow!), Bird (overrides: Tweet!):

Create Animal[] with one of each. Loop and call makeSound() on each.

Expected output:
Rex: Woof!
Whiskers: Meow!
Tweety: Tweet!
exercises.starter_code:
// Create Animal, Dog, Cat, Bird
// Put all in Animal[] and call makeSound() on each

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Rex: Woof!
Whiskers: Meow!
Tweety: Tweet!

hints: Animal[] can hold Dog, Cat, Bird (they are all Animals)
a.makeSound() calls each object's OWN makeSound
Even though reference is Animal type, the actual object's method runs
This is POLYMORPHISM — more on it next topic

exercises.title: Fix access violation — private field in subclass
exercises.scenario: Private fields aren't accessible in subclasses.
exercises.instructions:
This code doesn't compile. Fix it using the appropriate getter.

Expected output:
Rex is 3 years old

bugs.issue: `name` and `age` are private in Animal — Dog cannot access them directly.
bugs.fix: Use inherited getters: getName() and getAge()

hints: Private fields are hidden even from subclasses
Use the public getters: getName() and getAge()
These getters ARE inherited — you can call them from Dog

exercises.title: Account hierarchy
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `Account`: private balance (double), accountId (static auto-assign). Constructor validates balance >= 0. deposit(amount), getBalance(), getAccountId().
- `SavingsAccount extends Account`: private interestRate (double). Method applyInterest() adds interest to balance (balance * rate).

In main:
- Create savings account with ₹5000 and 5% interest
- Apply interest
- Print new balance

Expected output:
Account #1 balance after interest: ₹5250.0
hints: SavingsAccount constructor: super(balance) first
applyInterest uses getBalance() (inherited) to read balance
deposit() (inherited) to add the interest
5000 × 5% = 250 interest → 5250 total

exercises.title: Override toString() in hierarchy
exercises.scenario: Subclass toString extends parent's with additional info.
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `Product`: private id (final, static auto), name, price. toString: 'Product[#id name ₹price]'.
- `DigitalProduct extends Product`: private fileSize (String). Override toString: append ' (download: fileSize)' to parent's.
- `PhysicalProduct extends Product`: private weight (double). Override toString: append ' (ships: weightkg)' to parent's.

In main, create one of each and print them.

Expected output:
Product[#1 Java Book ₹599.0] (download: 25MB)
Product[#2 Keyboard ₹1999.0] (ships: 0.8kg)
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.Objects;

// Create Product, DigitalProduct, PhysicalProduct
hints: super(name, price) in each subclass constructor
super.toString() reuses Product's format
Each subclass appends its own info after

exercises.title: Trace override resolution
exercises.scenario: Which version runs when override exists?
exercises.expected_solution:
Hello from A
Hello from B
Hello from B
Hello from B

test_cases.expected:
Hello from A
Hello from B
Hello from B
Hello from B

hints: A uses A's greet
B uses B's greet (overrides A's)
C has no greet, looks up chain → finds B's
A ref = new B() — the OBJECT is B, so B's greet runs

exercises.title: Complete vehicle hierarchy
exercises.scenario: Three-class hierarchy with fields, overrides, and super.
exercises.instructions:
Build:
- `Vehicle`: year (int), make (String), model (String), protected double fuelEfficiency. toString: 'make model (year)'.
- `Car extends Vehicle`: private int numDoors. Override toString: append ', numDoors doors'.
- `ElectricCar extends Car`: private int batteryRange (km). Override toString: append ', range: rangeKm'.

All use super() in constructors and super.toString() in overrides.

Expected output:
Toyota Camry (2022)
Honda Civic (2023), 4 doors
Tesla Model 3 (2024), 4 doors, range: 500km

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Toyota Camry (2022)
Honda Civic (2023), 4 doors
Tesla Model 3 (2024), 4 doors, range: 500km

hints: ElectricCar calls Car's constructor (super) which calls Vehicle's
ElectricCar.toString calls super.toString (Car's) which calls super.toString (Vehicle's)
Chain of toString: Vehicle's format + Car's addition + ElectricCar's addition

exercises.title: Employee hierarchy with salary calculation
exercises.instructions:
Build:
- `Employee`: name, baseSalary. `calculateSalary()` returns baseSalary.
- `Manager extends Employee`: bonus (double). Override `calculateSalary()`: base + bonus.
- `SalesEmployee extends Employee`: commissionRate (double), salesAmount (double). Override `calculateSalary()`: base + salesAmount * commissionRate.

In main, create one of each. Store in Employee[]. Loop and print each employee's name + calculated salary.

Expected output:
Aisha: ₹75000.0
Raj: ₹100000.0
Priya: ₹88500.0
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Employee, Manager, SalesEmployee
// calculateSalary() is overridden in each subclass
hints: Manager: base + bonus = 85000 + 15000 = 100000
Employee[] holds all three types — polymorphism!
Each e.calculateSalary() calls the CORRECT override for that object

exercises.title: Notification system hierarchy
exercises.instructions:
Build a notification system:

1. `Notification` (base): message (String), priority (int, 1-5). Constructor validates. `send()` prints '[PRIORITY-n] message'. `getLog()` returns 'Notification: message'.

2. `EmailNotification extends Notification`: recipientEmail. Override `send()`: calls super.send() THEN prints 'Sent to: email'. Override `getLog()`: prepends 'Email | ' to super's.

3. `SMSNotification extends Notification`: phoneNumber. Similar overrides.

In main:
- Create one Email and one SMS notification
- Call send() on each
- Print getLogs

Expected output:
[PRIORITY-1] Server is down!
Sent to: ops@company.com
Log: Email | Notification: Server is down!
[PRIORITY-3] Reminder: meeting at 3pm
Sent to: +919876543210
Log: SMS | Notification: Reminder: meeting at 3pm
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Notification, EmailNotification, SMSNotification
// Use super in both send() and getLog()

test_cases.expected_stdout:
[PRIORITY-1] Server is down!
Sent to: ops@company.com
Log: Email | Notification: Server is down!
[PRIORITY-3] Reminder: meeting at 3pm
Sent to: +919876543210
Log: SMS | Notification: Reminder: meeting at 3pm

hints: Notification.send() handles the core output
EmailNotification.send() calls super.send() then adds email line
getLog() uses string prepending to parent's result
This pattern is common in real notification frameworks
